evals.prompt: Record an architectural decision: we'll store service configuration in TOML rather than JSON, because operators edit these files by hand and need comments and trailing-comma tolerance. We considered JSON (ubiquitous but no comments), YAML (comments, but whitespace-sensitive and error-prone for operators), and TOML. We chose TOML.
evals.expected_output: A small ADR created in docs/decisions via adg (add -> edit --from-stdin -> decide), with TOML as the chosen option. `adg validate` passes. No template scaffolding left in the accepted file.

assertions.description: An ADR NNNN-slug.md file exists under docs/decisions
assertions.check: docs/decisions/ contains at least one NNNN-slug.md file matching the decision

assertions.description: Content was created with adg commands, not by hand-editing the markdown
assertions.check: summary mentions adg add / adg edit --from-stdin / adg decide (no direct file authoring)

assertions.description: TOML is the chosen option in the Decision Outcome
assertions.check: the accepted ADR's Decision Outcome line names TOML

assertions.description: adg validate passes — no leftover {...} tokens, no empty required sections
assertions.check: adg validate --model docs/decisions exits zero; the file contains no template placeholder tokens

assertions.description: The ADR stays decision-focused (no Pros/Cons matrix or Confirmation dump for a clear-cut call)
assertions.check: the file is roughly within the small form factor (well under ~60 lines)

evals.prompt: I notice every outbound HTTP client in the codebase wraps calls in retry-with-exponential-backoff-and-jitter, and new code keeps reinventing it slightly differently. Should this be an ADR? If so, write it — the pattern exists because the upstream APIs rate-limit aggressively and uniform jitter avoids thundering-herd retries.
evals.expected_output: The skill affirms this warrants an ADR (a consistent pattern future authors must follow), then creates one in docs/decisions capturing the why (rate limits, thundering-herd) and the chosen approach (a single shared backoff+jitter helper). adg validate passes.

assertions.description: Agent explicitly decides the pattern is worth recording
assertions.check: summary contains an affirmative judgment that this warrants an ADR

assertions.description: An ADR NNNN-slug.md file exists under docs/decisions
assertions.check: docs/decisions/ contains at least one NNNN-slug.md file matching the decision

assertions.description: ADR records the driver (rate limiting / thundering herd)
assertions.check: the ADR Context or Drivers mention rate limiting or thundering-herd retries

evals.prompt: We're weighing how to deliver large files out of the worker without OOMing: keep the current in-memory buffer, stream to a temp file, or add a capability-flag opt-in for streaming. We haven't decided yet — capture the open decision so it's on record while we prototype.
evals.expected_output: A proposed (undecided) ADR in docs/decisions with the three options recorded and Decision Outcome left as the {...} placeholder. No `adg decide` is run. adg validate passes (proposed stubs are exempt from the finished-record checks).

assertions.description: An ADR NNNN-slug.md file exists under docs/decisions
assertions.check: docs/decisions/ contains at least one NNNN-slug.md file matching the decision

assertions.description: The ADR is left unresolved (status proposed, outcome is a placeholder)
assertions.check: the file's status is proposed and Decision Outcome is {...}; adg decide was not run

assertions.description: All three considered options are present
assertions.check: Considered Options lists the in-memory buffer, temp-file streaming, and capability-flag options

assertions.description: adg validate passes for the proposed stub
